proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between three leading continuous-treatment difference-in-differences estimators for the Average Causal Response (ACR) curve: the nonparametric continuous-treatment DiD of D'Haultfœuille, Hoderlein, Sasaki (2023, Journal of Econometrics) that estimates the marginal ACR via quantile-quantile matching between treatment-intensity strata, the de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfœuille (2024) generalized parallel trends estimator that targets the ACR via local-linear weighting in (cohort, treatment-dose) cells, and the panel TWFE-with-continuous-treatment estimator (the empirical default) that regresses outcome on the interaction of cohort and dose. All three target the population ACR functional ACR(d) = d/dd E[Y(d) - Y(0)] under generalized parallel trends + monotone dose-response. The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published continuous-DiD estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in (dose-distribution heterogeneity, cohort-time variation, parallel-trend bandwidth) space at which the three estimators have asymptotically equivalent identifying functionals versus strictly different ACR-limit functionals. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the three estimators identify the same ACR(d) at every interior dose d and achieve identical asymptotic variance if and only if a heterogeneity condition kappa(d) := lambda_max(Sigma_dose(d) Pi_cohort) <= kappa_star holds for every d in the interior of the dose support, where kappa_star is a sharp threshold equal to the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel matrix combining the dose-distribution covariance, the cohort-period treatment-share matrix, and the parallel-trend smoothness bandwidth; when kappa(d) > kappa_star on a non-trivial dose set, the DHS quantile-matching estimator achieves the semiparametric efficiency bound, the dCDH local-linear estimator is consistent but inefficient by a factor proportional to the bandwidth-misspecification rate, and the TWFE estimator is biased proportional to the cohort-dose interaction excess. kappa_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments + cohort-period treatment shares + parallel-trend bandwidth, NOT an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers TWFE consistency under homogeneous dose-cohort assignment (kappa(d)=0 for all d) and provides the first sharp triple-non-equivalence boundary for continuous-treatment DiD ACR identification in closed form.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains adjacent rather than duplicative: doc/API.md §3 has no accepted continuous-DID support-gap ACR theorem; pid_dose_response_iv_v1 concerns IV/rank-slice continuous-dose slopes, pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 concerns bounded anticipation support functions, and Q1 panel entries concern discrete history-cell FWL/sign-flip results. The gap is a partial-ID ACR derivative envelope for no-stayer/no-quasi continuous DID with common-intercept support-gap pasting.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are DCDHK2026NoUntreated, DCDHVB2024NoStayers, KimKwonKwonLee2018, Wells1973, LeGruyer2009, and DaniilidisHaddouLeGruyerLey2018. v5 corrects the key comparison: DCDHK's no-QUG parallel-trends WAS region is unbounded, while this proposal studies a different ACR(d0) target under monotone C^{1,1} restrictions and asks for an algorithmic clipped-arc identified set strictly sharper than the proposal's own [0,B] derivative baseline on a rho-open class."}
seed_list: Prior work DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023 would imply that rank-stationary quantile matching identifies a continuous-dose ACR without functional-form restrictions. Our seed conjectures that this functional coincides with dCDH switcher slopes and TWFE only on an observable rank-zero dose-cohort frontier, because cohort-specific dose heterogeneity creates active singular directions invisible to one-method identification.

Prior work CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 would predict that continuous-treatment TWFE difficulty is a decomposition problem. Our seed conjectures a sharper signed residual row: TWFE collapses to a causal ACR derivative if and only if residualized dose weights annihilate the dCDH switcher-score row, because dose-cohort covariance can mimic causal slope variation.

Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024 would imply that no-stayer continuous DiD can be handled by near-zero controls or parametric repair. Our seed conjectures that near-zero bandwidth controls alter the ACR score unless a spectral bandwidth operator is null, because local support gaps change the derivative target before estimation error enters.

Prior work RambachanRoth2023 would imply that structured parallel-trend deviations are sensitivity constraints around a fixed estimand. Our seed conjectures that in continuous-dose ACR DiD the bandwidth restriction can change the estimand itself, because the derivative score lives in a dose-local tangent space not present in binary event studies.

Prior work FreyaldenhovenHansenShapiro2019 would imply that pre-event proxies discipline counterfactual trends. Our seed conjectures that proxy-adjusted pre-period residuals are diagnostic but not score-preserving for ACR(d) unless they lie in the null space of the dose-local kernel, because proxy variation can rotate the local dose derivative.

Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 would imply that TWFE contamination is a weighted-average issue. Our seed conjectures a continuous-dose sign-reversal frontier where all marginal dose effects are monotone positive but the residualized TWFE ACR derivative is negative, because local dose density heterogeneity creates signed derivative weights rather than signed 2x2 comparisons.

Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022 would imply that switcher-stayer local slopes identify weighted ACR objects under same-baseline comparisons. Our seed conjectures that DHS rank-slice slopes and dCDH switcher slopes separate when same-baseline stayers exist but quantile ranks move across cohorts, because rank transport and baseline-dose conditioning are noncommuting operations.

Prior work deChaisemartinCicciaDHaultfoeuilleKnau2024 would imply that without true controls one can use near-zero-dose controls if support is available. Our seed conjectures sharp partial-identification bounds for ACR(d) when the near-zero window is empty, because the least-favorable derivative curve can be pasted at support-gap endpoints.

Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2022 would imply that weakly higher dose event-study estimators handle dynamic continuous treatments. Our seed conjectures that current-dose ACR and lagged-dose ACR share the three-method frontier only when the dose-path support operator factorizes by lag, because current and lagged derivative scores otherwise contaminate one another.

Prior work SunAbraham2021 would imply that event-study contamination arises from effects of other periods. Our seed conjectures a continuous-dose analogue where TWFE event-study coefficients contaminate ACR(d) through lagged dose derivatives even without negative binary weights, because derivative weights can load on off-dose histories.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023 would imply that rank-stationary quantile matching identifies a continuous-dose ACR without functional-form restrictions. Our seed conjectures that this functional coincides with dCDH switcher slopes and TWFE only on an observable rank-zero dose-cohort frontier, because cohort-specific dose heterogeneity creates active singular directions invisible to one-method identification.

anchor_paper.author: D'Haultfoeuille, Hoderlein, Sasaki
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies continuous-treatment causal effects using crossing treatment CDFs, stationarity, and rank invariance.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the extension exposes pairwise equality failure and a rank-zero iff frontier, not a broader assumption class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DHS plus dCDH because both identify ACR(d) under generalized parallel trends.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the methods condition on different primitives, rank transport versus same-baseline switcher support, and the literature gives no three-method equality certificate.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 would predict that continuous-treatment TWFE difficulty is a decomposition problem. Our seed conjectures a sharper signed residual row: TWFE collapses to a causal ACR derivative if and only if residualized dose weights annihilate the dCDH switcher-score row, because dose-cohort covariance can mimic causal slope variation.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway, Goodman-Bacon, Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Shows continuous-treatment TWFE parameters can be hard to interpret even with two periods.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/decomposition pattern, not blocked: refutes a named TWFE workflow and promotes the obstruction to an observable residual-row iff condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Goodman-Bacon weights because TWFE is always a weighted average.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Goodman-Bacon is binary timing; this seed ties continuous derivative weights to the dCDH ACR score row.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024 would imply that no-stayer continuous DiD can be handled by near-zero controls or parametric repair. Our seed conjectures that near-zero bandwidth controls alter the ACR score unless a spectral bandwidth operator is null, because local support gaps change the derivative target before estimation error enters.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Vazquez-Bare
anchor_paper.claim: Develops continuous-treatment DiD without stayers and notes TWFE limitations under heterogeneity and linearity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: bandwidth changes the estimand and creates a spectral null condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from kernel bandwidth bias theory because local linear estimators change with bandwidth.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the claim is about identification-score equality across named DiD functionals, not smoothing bias alone.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 would imply that structured parallel-trend deviations are sensitivity constraints around a fixed estimand. Our seed conjectures that in continuous-dose ACR DiD the bandwidth restriction can change the estimand itself, because the derivative score lives in a dose-local tangent space not present in binary event studies.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan, Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Develops robust inference for DiD/event-study designs under structured parallel-trend deviations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Generalizes parallel-trends pattern, not blocked: paired with the score-changing dose-local tangent-space phenomenon.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from honest DiD support functions because restrictions always change identified sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: honest DiD treats binary/event-time contrasts; it does not classify when a continuous ACR score is unchanged.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FreyaldenhovenHansenShapiro2019 would imply that pre-event proxies discipline counterfactual trends. Our seed conjectures that proxy-adjusted pre-period residuals are diagnostic but not score-preserving for ACR(d) unless they lie in the null space of the dose-local kernel, because proxy variation can rotate the local dose derivative.

anchor_paper.author: Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, Shapiro
anchor_paper.claim: Uses pre-event observables to construct counterfactual trends in event-study panels.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: names proxy-induced score rotation, not a generic extra covariate extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from proxy-control omitted-variable algebra.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed null-space condition concerns equality of ACR derivative scores across estimators, not only omitted variables.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 would imply that TWFE contamination is a weighted-average issue. Our seed conjectures a continuous-dose sign-reversal frontier where all marginal dose effects are monotone positive but the residualized TWFE ACR derivative is negative, because local dose density heterogeneity creates signed derivative weights rather than signed 2x2 comparisons.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE with staggered timing equals a weighted average of two-group/two-period DiD estimators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample pattern, not blocked: refutes named TWFE workflow on an open class with monotone positive marginal effects.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already known from negative weights in binary TWFE.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: binary 2x2 weights do not deliver a continuous-dose derivative sign-reversal frontier or ACR target comparison.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022 would imply that switcher-stayer local slopes identify weighted ACR objects under same-baseline comparisons. Our seed conjectures that DHS rank-slice slopes and dCDH switcher slopes separate when same-baseline stayers exist but quantile ranks move across cohorts, because rank transport and baseline-dose conditioning are noncommuting operations.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Pasquier, Sow, Vazquez-Bare
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies switchers' potential-outcome slopes using stayers with the same baseline treatment.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: the noncommutation of rank transport and baseline conditioning is a named mechanism.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because DHS and dCDH target different samples.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the seed isolates an equality/separation boundary when support overlaps and the sample distinction alone is insufficient.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work deChaisemartinCicciaDHaultfoeuilleKnau2024 would imply that without true controls one can use near-zero-dose controls if support is available. Our seed conjectures sharp partial-identification bounds for ACR(d) when the near-zero window is empty, because the least-favorable derivative curve can be pasted at support-gap endpoints.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin, Ciccia, D'Haultfoeuille, Knau
anchor_paper.claim: For heterogeneous adoption without stayers, uses near-zero-dose controls when available and bounds or parametric restrictions otherwise.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-identification boundary, not blocked: support-gap endpoints change sharp bounds, not merely estimator bandwidth.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a standard Manski support-bound calculation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the object is a derivative ACR curve under continuous DiD support gaps, not a level mean with missing outcomes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2022 would imply that weakly higher dose event-study estimators handle dynamic continuous treatments. Our seed conjectures that current-dose ACR and lagged-dose ACR share the three-method frontier only when the dose-path support operator factorizes by lag, because current and lagged derivative scores otherwise contaminate one another.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille
anchor_paper.claim: Proposes event-study estimators for effects of exposure to a weakly higher treatment dose over multiple periods.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends dynamic setting, not blocked: lag-factorization creates a failure boundary and new contamination term.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Sun-Abraham dynamic contamination with continuous treatments.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: it compares current and lagged dose derivative scores and three named continuous-treatment functionals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SunAbraham2021 would imply that event-study contamination arises from effects of other periods. Our seed conjectures a continuous-dose analogue where TWFE event-study coefficients contaminate ACR(d) through lagged dose derivatives even without negative binary weights, because derivative weights can load on off-dose histories.

anchor_paper.author: Sun, Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Event-study leads and lags can be contaminated by treatment effects from other periods under heterogeneous dynamic effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: continuous derivative weights expose contamination hidden in binary event-time coefficients.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already Sun-Abraham with continuous treatment labels.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Sun-Abraham does not characterize ACR(d) derivative contamination or DHS/dCDH/TWFE equivalence.

proposed_from.literature_map: Athey and Imbens (2006, Econometrica, AtheyImbens2006, doi:10.1111/j.1468-0262.2006.00668.x) anchor nonlinear changes-in-changes. DHS (2023, Journal of Econometrics, DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2022.07.003) identify continuous-treatment effects through CDF crossing, stationarity, and rank invariance. dCDHPSVB (2022/2025 arXiv, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022, https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.06898) identify switcher slopes using stayers at the same baseline treatment. dCDHV (2024, AEA P&P, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024, doi:10.1257/pandp.20241049) extend continuous-treatment DiD without stayers and warn against TWFE under heterogeneity. Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna (2024, NBER, CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024, doi:10.3386/w32117) show continuous-treatment TWFE parameters are difficult to interpret even with two periods. Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics, GoodmanBacon2021, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014) supplies the TWFE decomposition template. de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020, doi:10.1257/aer.20181169) show negative-weight TWFE behavior under heterogeneous effects. Sun-Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics, SunAbraham2021, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.09.006) show event-study contamination. Rambachan-Roth (2023, ReStud, RambachanRoth2023, doi:10.1093/restud/rdad018) motivate bandwidth/sensitivity geometry. Freyaldenhoven-Hansen-Shapiro (2019, AER, FreyaldenhovenHansenShapiro2019, doi:10.1257/aer.20180609) motivate pre-trend/proxy restrictions. The open tension is the absence of a sharp equality frontier among quantile-matching ACR, switcher/local-linear ACR, and residualized TWFE projection limits.

Continuous-DiD ACR non-equivalence kernel briefly recognized as flagship axis-b but proposer couldn't tighten angle 0 within cap, then degraded across pivots. Math sound at field tier.
